doc_title: LLaMA Beyond English: An Empirical Study on Language Capability Transfer

items.evidence_span: researchers in Natural Language Processing (NLP) have been exploring the fundamental principles of intelligence (Bubeck et al. 2023)
many LLMs like ChatGPT (OpenAI 2022)
PaLM (Anil et al. 2023)
LLaMA (Touvron et al. 2023a)
reasoning (Cobbe et al. 2021)
planning (Huang et al. 2022)
learning from experience (Dong et al. 2023)
Uniform Bar Examination (UBE) (Katz et al. 2023)
coding based on natural language instructions (StabilityAI 2023)
collecting extensive training data for all languages is nearly impossible (Ranta and Goutte 2021)
Taking the representative LLM BLOOM (Scao et al. 2023) as an example
Some researchers collect large-scale data for specific languages of interest and retrain an LLM (Team 2023a)
While Cui, Yang, and Yao (2023b) extend original vocabulary and further pretrain LLaMA with 30B Chinese tokens by LoRA (Hu et al. 2021)

items.fact: LLaMA (Touvron et al. 2023a) is a series of foundation models developed by Meta AI.
items.evidence_span: LLaMA (Touvron et al. 2023a): LLaMA is a series of foundation models developed by Meta AI

items.fact: LLaMA2 (Touvron et al. 2023b) is an enhanced and upgraded version of LLaMA.
items.evidence_span: LLaMA2 (Touvron et al. 2023b): LLaMA2 is an enhanced and upgraded version of LLaMA.

items.fact: Chinese LLaMA (Cui, Yang, and Yao 2023b) is an extension of the original LLaMA.
items.evidence_span: Chinese LLaMA (Cui, Yang, and Yao 2023b): Chinese LLaMA is an extension of the original LLaMA

items.fact: Chinese LLaMA2 (Cui, Yang, and Yao 2023a) is an advanced iteration of Chinese LLaMA.
items.evidence_span: Chinese LLaMA2 (Cui, Yang, and Yao 2023a): Chinese LLaMA2 is an advanced iteration of Chinese LLaMA.
BELLE (Ji et al. 2023): BELLE is a large-scale Chinese instruction tuning dataset developed by Lianjia Tech.
Bactrian-X (Li et al. 2023): Bactrian-X contains instructions and responses across 52 languages
It is created by translating $67 \mathrm{~K}$ English instructions from Alpaca-52k (Taori et al. 2023)
and Dolly-15k (Conover et al. 2023) datasets into 51 languages
LLM-Eval (Zhang et al. 2023a): LLM-Eval is a manually constructed benchmark for instruction-following evaluation.
C-Eval (Huang et al. 2023b): C-Eval is a Chinese evaluation suite with 13948 exam questions across 52 subjects
MMLU (Hendrycks et al. 2020): MMLU measures a LLM's ability to learn and apply knowledge across 57 diverse subjects
AGI-Eval (Zhong et al. 2023): AGIEval uses questions from standardized tests taken by millions of people
Gaokao-Bench (Zhang et al. 2023b): GAOKAO-Bench uses 2811 exam questions from Chinese college entrance exams

items.fact: The paper references Zhang et al. (2023a) for the LLM-Eval evaluation protocol.
items.evidence_span: For LLM-Eval, we followed the practice of Zhang et al. (2023a)
